title: Repeated thefts, CCTV clips, and family ties: Inside Ram Temple donation 'embezzlement' probe
content: A Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe into alleged theft from Ayodhya's Ram Janmabhoomi temple donation boxes has led to eight arrests and the recovery of over ₹79 lakh. CCTV footage reportedly shows an accused repeatedly stealing cash, with allegations of a cover-up involving a relative of a co-accused. Trust leadership has resigned, and Chief Minister Adityanath vows no one will be spared in the ongoing investigation.

title: 589 dead, nearly 3,000 injured: Venezuela quake toll rises as rescuers race to find survivors
content: Venezuela grapples with a devastating toll of 589 dead and nearly 3,000 injured following twin earthquakes. La Guaira state is severely impacted, with thousands missing and millions potentially affected. International rescue teams and aid, including India's 'Operation Amistad' and US assistance, are now arriving to support desperate search-and-rescue efforts amidst widespread destruction and fear of aftershocks.

title: Supreme Court 'allows' US Border Patrol agents to deport some Green Card holders: Check who
content: US border officials can now deport certain Green Card holders more easily following a Supreme Court ruling. The court decided that authorities don't need definitive proof of a crime involving moral turpitude before treating returning lawful permanent residents as new applicants for entry. This change simplifies the process for initiating removal proceedings against Green Card holders under specific circumstances, impacting their status upon re-entry.

title: Watch: Boeing 777's near-miss — wing skims inches from runway during risky manoeuvre
content: The widely shared footage has fuelled debate among aviation experts and pilots, many of whom questioned the unusually low altitude at which the widebody aircraft was flown. The Boeing 777 is one of the world's largest twin-engine commercial aircraft and is generally operated with generous safety margins during low-level manoeuvres because of its size, weight and performance characteristics.

title: 'Sarpanch' Shreyas breaks Ro-Ko's record in 1st game as India captain, becomes ...
content: When he walked out for the toss against Ireland in Belfast on Friday, Iyer had already captained 114 T20 matches across franchise and domestic cricket — the most by an Indian before taking charge of the national side in the format.

title: Wig conspiracy behind Ketan's murder? Father says Siya Goyal’s family knew about his hair patch
content: The father of murdered Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal met the Maharashtra CM, demanding the death penalty for the accused. The CM assured a fast-tracked investigation and an SIT formation. Police revealed the victim was pushed from Lohagad Fort after the accused allegedly feared family disrepute if they eloped. Both suspects have confessed to the conspiracy and murder.

title: Bill Gates admits to the House panel that he had at least three extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to Congress that Jeffrey Epstein attempted to leverage knowledge of Gates' extramarital affairs to re-establish contact after Gates sought to end their association. While no direct threats were made, Epstein allegedly used intermediaries for veiled warnings and explored blackmail tactics. Gates acknowledged past affairs, emphasizing Epstein's lack of involvement and that he only learned of them post-professional ties.

title: 'Unfortunately, no': Iyer confirms Sooryavanshi will have to wait for India debut
content: Young batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will have to wait for his historic India debut. Captain Shreyas Iyer confirmed the 15-year-old was not in the playing XI for the first T20I against Ireland, citing team balance.  Despite the delay, Sooryavanshi, who had a stellar IPL and India A season, remains on the cusp of becoming India's youngest international cricketer.

title: Telangana 2BHK housing scheme: Who gets a free house and how the selection works
content: Telangana's Double Bedroom Housing Scheme offers fully constructed, free homes to the underprivileged and homeless communities. Each residence spans 560 sq ft, complete with necessary amenities, lifting financial constraints for recipients. Focused on Below Poverty Line families, the initiative ensures ample reservations for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and minorities, employing a detailed multi-step verification and selection process for fair distribution.

title: Investor Michael Burry repeats warning on Palantir after calling stock ‘a sand castle’
content: Renowned investor Michael Burry has expressed serious concerns about Palantir Technologies, likening its stock to a "sand castle" amid a significant downturn.  He noted a "new lower low" with declining trading volume, suggesting a prolonged downtrend.  This comes as Palantir, once an AI darling, faces investor skepticism over its valuation.  Meanwhile, Burry has increased his stake in China's Alibaba, seeing it as an undervalued AI leader.

title: Ram Temple donation row: Rs 80 lakh recovered during probe; all 8 accused sent to 3-day judicial custody
content: Eight individuals arrested in connection with the alleged theft of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Temple donations have been remanded to judicial custody until June 29. Investigators have recovered nearly Rs 80 lakh believed to be linked to the embezzled funds. Preliminary findings suggest six of the accused, employed by a bank's outsourced cash management agency, were directly involved in counting the donations when the alleged misappropriation occurred.

title: The NBA's next search isn't for a superstar. It's for the next big idea
content: Artificial intelligence, player health, fan engagement and startup innovation are quietly becoming part of the NBA's long-term strategy. At the Rising Stars Invitational in Singapore, the league unveiled a dedicated Asia track for NBA Launchpad, signalling a shift from viewing the region as merely a market to seeing it as a source of ideas that could redefine basketball on and off the court.

title: 'Maha dacoity at Ram Temple': Kejriwal claims 'powerful demons' involved in theft; CM Yogi hits back
content: Aam Aadmi Party chief Arvind Kejriwal has accused the Uttar Pradesh government of shielding powerful individuals in the alleged embezzlement of Ram Mandir donations. He claimed a "maha dacoity" occurred, with influential figures involved and the investigation being controlled by the accused. Meanwhile, UP CM Yogi Adityanath criticized Kejriwal's visit, stating Delhi has received only corruption from him.

title: Watch: Drunk man climbs 11,000-volt pole, power supply shut for rescue
content: Panic gripped Maharajganj's Parsa Khurd village after a man, allegedly drunk, climbed an 11,000-volt electricity pole. Villagers watched in fear as the man remained perched for hours, disrupting power supply. Authorities swiftly cut power and launched a risky rescue, safely bringing him down. He later became aggressive, leading to an FIR for endangering public safety.

title: Ben Stokes scripts history, becomes only second after Jacques Kallis to...
content: Ben Stokes made a triumphant return to Test cricket, achieving a historic all-round milestone of over 7,000 runs and 250 wickets, a feat matched only by Jacques Kallis.  His impactful bowling display against New Zealand, including a crucial breakthrough and four wickets, revitalized England's efforts after a challenging start. This performance followed a brief suspension for a curfew breach, showcasing Stokes' immediate influence on the field.

title: Brittany Mahomes reveals how Patrick Mahomes and motherhood changed her forever
content: Brittany Mahomes shared that becoming a mother to Sterling, Bronze, and Golden has completely changed her outlook on life. She said motherhood made her more intentional, purposeful, and motivated to inspire her children to dream big and work hard. Brittany now prioritizes opportunities that align with her family values. Patrick Mahomes has also spoken about balancing his NFL career with being a devoted husband and father, emphasizing that family always comes first.

title: Has US-Iran deal erased Middle East pain for India inc?
content: Global market jitters over Middle East tensions have eased following a US-Iran ceasefire, improving the outlook for Indian corporate profitability. Crisil Ratings now anticipates a smaller 100-basis-point decline in operating margins for fiscal 2027, down from a previous 200-basis-point estimate. While most sectors are expected to see minimal disruption, some like airlines and specialty chemicals remain under pressure due to persistent input cost challenges.

title: US eyes 2,000‑km air‑to‑air missile, poised to rewrite aerial warfare rules
content: The US Air Force is developing a groundbreaking long-range missile, the AFLRW, capable of striking targets over 1,850 km away. This initiative aims to counter China's growing missile capabilities, particularly in the vast Pacific theater. The program prioritizes air-to-air variants, envisioning a future where advanced sensor networks, not just aircraft radar, guide these powerful weapons to their targets, redefining aerial combat.

title: India reign supreme! Shooters top ISSF Jr World C'ship medal tally in Germany
content: Team India has triumphed at the ISSF Junior World Championship 2026, topping the medal standings with an impressive 24 medals, including seven golds. Young talents shone across rifle and pistol disciplines, securing victories in individual, mixed, and team events. This remarkable achievement underscores India's growing prowess in world shooting, with the NRAI crediting effective grassroots programs for the widespread success.

title: Who is Tina Ball? Inside LaVar Ball's 30-year marriage and divorce
content: Tina Ball, the former college basketball player and longtime wife of LaVar Ball, is back in the spotlight after LaVar revealed they have ended their relationship following nearly 30 years together. Tina played basketball at California State University, Los Angeles, later worked in education and with Big Baller Brand, and remained a private figure while raising sons Lonzo, LiAngelo and LaMelo Ball. LaVar shared the news during a June 24 livestream, saying Tina "decided to go her own way" while expressing gratitude for the years they spent together.

title: 'Let's play like Lions': Shreyas Iyer's first message as India's new T20I captain
content: Shreyas Iyer is set to lead India's T20I side against Ireland, marking a significant return to the national setup with added captaincy responsibility.  Head coach Gautam Gambhir expressed confidence in Iyer's leadership, while the new skipper emphasized building a supportive and fun team culture. Iyer urged his teammates to play fearlessly and with belief, aiming to usher in a new era for the team.

title: Ketan Agrawal murder case: Siya is ‘spinning stories’ during interrogation, say police
content: The Ketan Agarwal murder case has taken a new turn as Lonavala police on Friday said the prime accused, Siya, has given conflicting statements during interrogation, while chats recovered from mobile phones appear to contradict her claims.

title: 'Even vice-captains get dropped': Ex-India cricketer's blunt warning to Tilak Varma
content: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif cautioned young Tilak Varma, newly appointed T20I vice-captain, that the role offers no job security. Kaif highlighted recent instances like Axar Patel and Shubman Gill losing their positions despite leadership roles, emphasizing that consistent performance is paramount in the current Indian team.  He stressed that every player, regardless of designation, must continually prove their worth to retain their spot in the playing XI.

title: Government can't claim citizens' land through adverse possession: Punjab and Haryana HC
content: The Punjab and Haryana High Court ruled that the state cannot claim ownership of private land through adverse possession, deeming it incompatible with a welfare state's role. Justice Ramesh Kumari ordered the Haryana government to compensate landowners for 7 Kanals of land used for an irrigation channel since 1960 without lawful acquisition.

title: Paris Diamond League in doubt as police seek cancellation due to historic heat wave
content: A historic heatwave has put this weekend's Paris Diamond League athletics meet in doubt. French authorities have requested its cancellation, citing immense pressure on emergency services and extreme weather.  While organisers initially planned a modified event for elite athletes, the police prefecture has warned of an enforced cancellation if organisers don't comply. This unprecedented heat has already broken national records.

title: Kangana Ranaut reacts to Ketan Agarwal death, says parents can't be blamed
content: A Pune man's death, initially ruled an accident, is now a murder investigation. His fiancée and her alleged partner are accused of his killing. Amidst public discussion, Kangana Ranaut commented on parental responsibility, highlighting the significant impact of social media, AI, and peer groups on individuals' choices, suggesting families shouldn't be solely blamed for their children's actions.

title: Gurgaon cop removed from duty after video shows him abusing auto driver who refused free ride
content: A home guard with Gurgaon Police faced immediate removal from duty after a viral video captured him allegedly hurling abuse at an auto-rickshaw driver. The incident reportedly began when the driver refused a free ride, citing a prior app booking. The driver's account suggests the officer boarded his auto and demanded a free trip. Gurgaon Police confirmed the action, initiating departmental proceedings and vowing further steps based on inquiry findings.

title: DU UG admissions 2026: CSAS portal registration likely to open on June 27, here's how to apply
content: Delhi University's undergraduate admissions for the 2026-27 academic year are set to commence with the CSAS portal opening on June 27.  Admissions will exclusively rely on CUET UG 2026 scores, offering over 71,000 seats across various programs. Students should prepare their documents and monitor the official portal for application steps, which involve registration, preference filling, and seat allotment rounds. The academic session begins July 21.

title: Not Sachin, Kohli: Aussie legend says Sooryavanshi was 1st cricketer he asked for selfie
content: If selected, Sooryavanshi will be 15 years and 91 days old, younger than Sachin Tendulkar was when he made his international debut against Pakistan in 1989 at the age of 16 years and 205 days.

title: Op Amistad: India deploys two C17s to quake-hit Venezuela with medical supplies, aid
content: India has launched 'Operation Amistad' to aid earthquake-stricken Venezuela, dispatching two Indian Air Force (IAF) C-17 aircraft. These planes carry a 41-member Army field hospital unit and over 35 tonnes of vital relief supplies, including medicines and advanced medical equipment like BHISHM Cubes. This humanitarian mission underscores India's commitment to supporting Venezuela during its challenging post-earthquake recovery efforts.

title: For first time, names of soldiers martyred during Operation Sindoor made public
content: Six brave Indian soldiers, including five from the Army and one from the Air Force, who sacrificed their lives during Operation Sindoor following the Pahalgam terror attack, have been identified. Their names will be permanently inscribed at the National War Memorial. The operation saw precision strikes against terror launchpads, eliminating over 100 terrorists and showcasing India's resolute stance against extremism.

title: Under Gambhir's watchful eye, Sooryavanshi unleashes fiery shots - Watch
content: Fifteen-year-old batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has trained with the senior Indian cricket team ahead of their T20I series against Ireland. Spotted practicing attacking shots under coach Gautam Gambhir, Sooryavanshi could become India's youngest international debutant, potentially breaking Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record. His inclusion presents a selection dilemma for the team management, balancing a winning combination with the excitement surrounding the young star.

title: Oldest-ever! Hugo Broos scripts WC history as SA reach knockouts for the first time
content: South Africa has made history by reaching the FIFA World Cup knockout stages for the first time, securing a crucial 1-0 win against South Korea. Coach Hugo Broos, at 74, became the oldest manager to win a World Cup match. Youngster Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive goal, showcasing the team's belief and tactical prowess. They now face Canada in the Round of 32.

title: Watch: Agra BJP corporator cuts birthday cake in drain to protest civic apathy
content: A BJP corporator in Agra staged a unique protest for his birthday, standing knee-deep in a filthy drain to highlight the municipal corporation's alleged neglect of drainage works.  Despite numerous complaints over three years, the drain remains unrepaired and uncleaned, impacting residents and leading to tragic incidents.  Local citizens are threatening further action if the issue isn't addressed.

title: Monsoon misfires: Over half of Telangana districts face rainfall stress, Kharif sowing at risk
content: Telangana faces significant rainfall stress, with 17 out of 33 districts experiencing deficient or largely deficient rainfall less than a month into the monsoon. This shortfall, particularly in key agricultural areas, raises concerns for kharif sowing. While the state's overall deficit is currently within normal limits, the uneven distribution and severe deficits in some districts highlight a growing regional imbalance, prompting calls for contingency plans.

title: Hybrid vs EV: Delhi GoM divided over subsidy plan in new mobility policy
content: Delhi's EV Policy 2.0 faces a hurdle as ministers debate incentives for strong hybrid vehicles. While some see them as a crucial stepping stone to cleaner transport, others insist on focusing solely on zero-emission electric cars. Hybrids, currently a significant part of Delhi's vehicle registrations, offer a compromise for those wary of EV range and cost, but experts argue for a complete shift to electric mobility to meet long-term goals.

title: NH-66: Expert panel proposes structural changes
content: Recommends replacing or extending vulnerable Reinforced Earth overbridges with pillar-supported structures at three critical locations

title: Sukhu reiterates Himachal’s claim to Chandigarh share, seeks Governor’s intervention
content: Chief Minister raises Chandigarh share, BBMB dues and Shanan project during meeting with Punjab Governor and UT Administrator

title: ‘Uyir’ movie review: A run-of-the-mill police procedural with a dated approach
content: The makers of Uyir have the advantage of working with a remarkable real story, with the possibilities of playing around with a plethora of human emotions. Unfortunately, it has not translated well on screen

title: State’s first waste to energy plant in Hubballi to be fully commissioned by January next
content: Trial run of the initiative taken up by Union Minister Pralhad Joshi when he was holding the Coal portfolio has been successfully conducted

title: Opposition in Thrissur Corporation seeks probe into ‘misuse’ of Deputy Mayor’s official vehicle
content: Visuals showing Youth Congress activists travelling in the car emerge; BJP seeks A. Prasad’s resignation

title: LDF drowned Kerala in liquor, says Minister O.J. Janeesh
content: Says UDF yet to finalise liquor policy; decision to be taken after consultations within front and party

title: Full restoration of safaris in Bandipur and Nagarahole tiger reserves
content: Safaris were suspended in Bandipur and Nagarahole since November 2025 in the wake of human-tiger conflict in the region

title: Bike taxi operators under scanner in Kozhikode
content: Use of privately registered motorcycles for commercial purposes without prescribed authorisation are liable to attract fines ranging from ₹2,000 to ₹5,000, in addition to other legal actions

title: Change in Saturday school timings leaves parents, teachers worried in Malnad area
content: The revision, aimed at standardising timings across the State, has created an impasse: uniformity on paper at conflict with the region’s lived geography.

title: All eight arrested in Ram temple donation row sent to police custody till Jun 29; ₹79.85 lakh recovered
content: As per the FIR, the accused were involved in the process of counting cash and valuables donated by devotees at the temple; officials alleged that they had misappropriated donations collected through boxes installed on the temple premises.

title: Tobacco Board cuts FCV crop target for A.P. to 81 million kg for 2026-27
content: Tobacco Board resolves to form a high-level panel comprising all stakeholders to recommend reforms in auction system, procurement mechanism, crop regulation and overall marketing framework

title: Punjab border residents oppose Himachal entry tax; Nihang Sikhs warn of renewed protest
content: Residents, transporters and traders demand rollback of Himachal Pradesh’s vehicle entry tax as legal challenge continues and Nihang Sikhs threaten to revive ‘Khalsa tax’ agitation

title: SEBI rejects Anil Ambani settlement request over Reliance Infrastructure
content: Anil Ambani, the younger brother ‌of billionaire Mukesh Ambani, has faced growing scrutiny from regulators and enforcement agencies over the past 18 months.

title: Decision on low-alcohol beverages solely based on liquor policy, says Kerala Excise Minister M. Liju
content: He says that the Budget proposal for tax cuts on low-alcohol beverages was considering this future possibility, and not a decision to be implemented immediately

title: Microbiology, technology vital for sustainable development and biosafety, says MLA
content: Gadde Ramamohan was speaking at the golden jubilee celebrations of the Department of Microbiology of Gulbarga University

title: Congress demands Supreme Court-monitored probe into alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement case
content: Exploiting Lord Ram for votes and notes is unacceptable, the Congress party seeks the disbanding of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust.

title: AGICL invites bids for consultant to oversee ₹4,933-crore Amaravati Secretariat works
content: The consultant will supervise the MEP, facade, interiors and allied works of the 145-department complex, with bids to be opened on June 30

title: ‘One Million Toofan Goals’ campaign in Kerala schools crosses target in 10 days
content: Penalty kicks were organised by setting up ‘Toofan goal posts’ in schools for children to ‘kick goals against drugs.’ Till Thursday, children in schools across the State scored 10,67,038 goals

title: ‘Ensure benefits of economic empowerment projects reach Bhovi community’
content: ‘Bhovi Development Corporation is planning to encourage the community and support it in dairy farming, cattle rearing and poultry farming’

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay flags off anti-drug marathon, runs to spread awareness
content: The Chief Minister signed on the commitment board saying ‘Sports-ah edu, drugs-ah vidu’ (Take up sports, give up drugs).

title: State waging uncompromising battle against narcotics: Minister P. C. Vishnunadh
content: He was speaking after flagging off an anti-drug awareness rally organised by the Kollam Rural Police at the Elampalloor SNSM Higher Secondary School in connection with the International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking

title: Loan waiver scheme will do injustice to 70% of Maharashtra farmers, says Rohit Pawar
content: Mr. Pawar warned of a large-scale agitation from June 29 if the government fails to fulfil the assurance about the 'Punyashlok Ahilyadevi Holkar Shetkari Karja-Mukti Yojana'

title: TTD to launch ‘Akshara Govindam’, ‘Annaprasana’ services at Vakula Matha temple on July 3
content: Both the services will be made available on all days from July 4, except Tuesdays, and advance registration is mandatory one hour ahead of the ceremony at the temple

title: A.P. Farmers’ Association alleges exploitation of oil palm growers, seeks new oil fed plant at Pedavegi
content: The association criticises oil palm companies for moving court against the government's decision to fix  Oil Extraction Rate at 20.5%17.5%.

title: Odisha govt. suspends four senior officials for 1,678 mistakes in new school textbooks
content: Former SCERT director among the four suspended officials; the SCERT will prepare a Master Errata Register and provide corrected information to all students, and establish a Quality Assurance Cell

title: Tourists continue dangerous acts on road in Munnar
content: Despite repeated warnings and police cases, they continue riding atop vehicles and with doors open on the winding roads of the hill town; viral video shows a group of youths riding their SUV dangerously near Chithirapuram

title: Arrangements in place for Pulse Polio drive in Kerala on June 28
content: Over 19 lakh children under the age of five years will be given oral polio drops in the State. For this, 22,288 booths will be set up and 46,663 trained volunteers will be deployed to administer the vaccine

title: T.N. Minister Sarathkumar says he was crushing child’s medicine, not drugs in viral video
content: The video recorded during an IPL match showed him using a credit or debit card to make a powder line with a white substance placed on a mobile phone screen

title: Pharma distributor’s licence cancelled over Kota postpartum deaths; WHO seeks report
content: Five women died between May 5 and May 17 at the JK Lone Hospital and the Super Speciality Wing of Government Medical College in Kota, Rajasthan, following Caesarean section deliveries

title: YSRCP alleges fertilizer shortage in Andhra Pradesh
content: While liquor is available even in remote areas, farmers are struggling to procure fertilizers, alleges party general secretary Nagi Reddy; he also demands withdrawal of app-based distribution system as poor Internet connectivity and digital payment issues are denying access to subsidised fertilizers in rural and Agency areas

title: Pune fort murder: CM Fadnavis assures justice to victim's kin, agrees to fast-track trial
content: Mr. Fadnavis also accepted the family's demand to try the case in a fast-track court and approved the appointment of senior advocate Ujjwal Nikam as the special public prosecutor in the case

title: India’s envoy to Bangladesh Dinesh Trivedi assumes office, announces resumption of travel visas
content: Visa applications can be submitted starting June 28, Mr. Trivedi said after presenting his credentials to Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin; Union govt. accords Mr. Trivedi the status of a Union Minister for ceremonial functions

title: Ram Mandir fund row: Temple Trust gen secy resigns on 'moral grounds'; trustee quits too
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." Read More

title: Iran’s biggest battle starts now: What lies ahead in the 60-day ceasefire window
content: Priyanshi Rastogi is a journalist with The Times of India. She primarily covers national and international news, along with business affairs. Her work focuses on the latest political events, in-depth explainers, features, offbeat articles and viral social media trending stories. Read More

title: Why South Africans Are Protesting Against Immigrants
content: Anti-foreigner groups say South Africa is overrun with illegal immigrants who take jobs away from South Africans, use up scarce public services and are responsible for high crime rates.

title: Incoming Missile Alert Sounded In Dubai, Then A "Disregard Warning" Message
content: Since the mobile alert was the first in weeks since the ceasefire between the United States and Iran took place, it startled the residents of Dubai.

title: 'Where Do We Go?' 310 Families Left On Streets After Yamuna Bazar Demolition
content: The families are homeless after the Delhi Development Authority (DDA) carried out a demolition drive in the Yamuna Bazar area.

title: Cops Throw Dummy Off Fort To Recreate Murder, Establish Siya Goyal's Role
content: The Pune police executed a physical recreation of the crime scene at the Lohagarh Fort, on the outskirts of Pune.

title: India's 1st Hydrogen Train Gets Approval. Here Is What Makes It Special
content: The train will have ten coaches and will be powered by a 1,200 kW hydrogen fuel cell propulsion system. It will run at a commercial speed of up to 75 km/h, though trial runs will be conducted at speeds of up to 120 km/h.

title: Earthquakes Hit Venezuela, But Tremors Could Be Felt 14,000 km Away In India
content: Power outages, damaged transport links and emergency restrictions at ports in Venezuela can slow cargo movements for days or even weeks.

title: AI Won't Trigger 'Job Apocalypse', May Create Uneven Disruption: Report
content: The report, titled An AI Job Apocalypse, brings together views from economists and AI experts, who broadly agree that while AI will displace workers, it is also likely to create new employment opportunities over time.

title: UN Demands Independent Probe Into ICE Custody Deaths In US
content: At least 52 deaths have been reported in ICE holding facilities since the start of 2025, when US President Donald Trump returned to office and launched a crackdown on immigrants, the UN human rights office said.

title: Woman Accused Of Poisoning, Killing 4 Grandchildren, Daughter In US
content: Amy Steadman, 64, has been accused of being responsible for the deaths of herself, her daughter, and the four minors, officers in Mechanicville, New York, said.

title: "If Passport Does Not Establish Citizenship, Then What Does?" Shashi Tharoor
content: Shashi Tharoor urged the government to amend the legal framework to make both the passport and the Aadhaar card valid and conclusive proof of Indian citizenship unless they are explicitly cancelled or withdrawn by the state.

title: AI Chips Worth $13 Million Seized At Malaysia's Kuala Lumpur Airport
content: Malaysia imposed export controls last year on the movement of high-performance chips of US origin, following pressure from the United States to stem the flow to China of chips crucial for the development of AI.

title: Family Rejects 'Wig Theory' As Motive For Ketan Agarwal's Killing
content: Ketan Agarwal, 25, a director and chief marketing officer in his family's real estate business, Success Group, died after falling from the fort.

title: 6.5-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Off Coast Of Philippines, No Tsunami Alert
content: The quake struck at a depth of 52.4 kilometres in the waters about 35 kilometres southwest of Mindanao island's Jose Abad Santos town.

title: 26/11 Prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam To Fight Pune Murder Victim's Case
content: In a post on X, Chief Minister's office, said Ketan Agarwal's father, Vishal Agarwal, met Devendra Fadnavis in Pune today and demanded justice for his son.

title: Venezuela Quake Death Count More Than Doubles to 589, Over 2,900 injured
content: Acting President Delcy Rodriguez made the announcement surrounded by government and military officials as she welcomed the arrival of rescue crews from all over the world.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Death Count In Venezuela Earthquake More Than Doubles To 589, Over 2,980 Injured
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: Ex-Bank Manager Duped Of Rs 2.2 Crore In Ghaziabad In Digital Arrest Scam
content: An 84-year-old retired bank manager and his wife were allegedly duped of Rs 2.2 crore by cyber fraudsters who kept them under a "digital arrest" for 12 days in Ghaziabad, police said.

title: Centre Will Amend NDPS Act To Crack Down On Drug Syndicates: Amit Shah
content: The home minister also spoke about "success achieved" by agencies in tackling the drug menace in the country.

title: 'Shouldn't Become Islamabad Suburb': Denmark Pushes To Ban Public 'Azaan'
content: Denmark's Immigration Minister Morten Bodskov said that the call to prayer should not be heard over Danish rooftops and that it should not seem that one has ended up in Islamabad while walking through the country.

title: Japan PM Sanae Takaichi To Visit India From July 1 to 3 For Annual Summit
content: At the invitation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Takaichi will be undertaking an official visit to New Delhi from July 1 to 3 for the 16th India-Japan annual summit, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said on Friday.
